rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY LYNCH VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expung Lis Pendens And For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 9, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT JUSTIN LUU, XIAO WU NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXPUNG LIS PENDENS AND FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to show probable validity of its real property claims. Defendant's request for attorney's fees is granted as requested. Plaintiff may seek leave of Court to record a new Lis Pendens if and when Plaintiff asserts a viable real property claim against the moving defendants. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY LYNCH VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 9, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action for wrongful eviction, trespass, conversion and Intentional Infliction of emotional distress to allege facts supporting tolling of the statue of limitations and to allege facts (not conclusions) supporting each element of these causes of action against Wells Fargo. Demurrer is sustained to all other causes of action to allege facts (not conclusions) to support each element of these causes of action in clear and concise language. Plaintiff must also allege tender or facts supporting an exception from the tender rule. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BONG ENTERPRISE, INC. VS. MARY BONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 9, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT MARY BONG, CHARLES CHUA, CHARLESTON CHUA, KINGSTON CHUA MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. The Court obtained jurisdiction upon the service of the summons. However, as defendant correctly points out, Plaintiff having failed to comply with CCP 1167, shall now proceed with "an ordinary action arising out of the tort or breach of contract." This case will be placed on the civil action case management track. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VS. KATYA ROSTOMYAN JORICH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Delcaration In Support Of Motion To Vacate Judgment; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 9, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT KATYA JORICH NOTICE OF MOTION, MOTION, MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND DELCARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT; PROPOSED ORDER is denied. Ms. Jorich provides no documentary support of any kind for her statement that she attended a naturalization ceremony on June 3, 2024, the sole factual basis for this motion, and that statement is disputed by plaintiff. Given this disputed fact and the high likelihood that documentary evidence is available to support Ms. Jorich's statement if that statement were true, the burden is on Ms. Ms. Jorich to provide such evidence. If Ms. Jorich chooses to contest this tentative ruling, she should bring documentary support that she attended a naturalization ceremony on June 3, 2024. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
